title: What are the two of you inside my heart
display_title: What are the two of you inside my heart
wikitext:
[]: "What are the two of you inside my heart?"             Quote Post by sensei_juice » Thu May 03, 2007 6:46 am Regardless of what Anno said (remember, Anno tends to be rather modest, so I'm not inclined to believe him when he calls everything pedantry), I've always interpreted this scene as religiously symbolic. Without turning this thread into a discussion about that issue, though, what are your thoughts on that scene? EDIT: I'm referring to: Shinji: But, what are you two within my heart? Rei: Hope. The hope that people might be able to understand one another. Kaworu: And the words 'I love you'.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Thu May 03, 2007 7:31 am I have no fucking clue what you're trying to open discussion on.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu May 03, 2007 8:13 am I assume he's talking about this: Shinji: But, what are you two within my heart? Rei: Hope. The hope that people might be able to understand one another. Kaworu: And the words 'I love you'. Hmmmmm, Kaworu/Rei, Adam/Lilith representing Love and Hope... The thing I found most interesting about this whole thing goes back to the Lake scene in 24' when Kaworu is talking and says: 04: There is a man trying to close the Box before the Hope at the bottom appears. KAWORU: Hope? You're saying that is the Lilim's hope? I always wondered if this was referencing Lilith (maybe in an offhand way?).

[]:             Quote Post by sensei_juice » Thu May 03, 2007 9:47 am Heh, sorry for being confusing - Eva Yojimbo, that's what I was talking about. Interesting - I need to rewatch 24'. And btw, my interpretation: Seele (institutionalized religion) uses Kaworu's dummy plugs (Jesus, but without a soul; arguably the idea of Jesus without his intent) to initiate Instrumentality; this is a misuse (or misinterpretation) of hope/love. Two ways to interpret that much: one, a specific comment that institutionalized religion has grown apart from what Jesus stood for; two, the idea that religion in general recognizes the loneliness of humanity, but misinterprets that hope/love into the desire for instrumentality (God, who does not actually exist in the Judeo-Christian sense). I prefer the second interpretation, but I recognize that views on religion are BYO here.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu May 03, 2007 10:49 am I'm pretty sure these scenes are being referenced here: #19 Shinji: Because I couldn't forgive my father. He betrayed me. I was finally able to have a comfortable conversation with my father, but he won't try to understand my feelings at all! Rei: Did you try to understand your father's feelings? Shinji: I tried. #20 Rei Why do you hate your father? Shinji: Of course! Anyone would. Rei: You can't understand your father? Shinji: Of course not. I've hardly ever seen him. Rei: Is that why you hate him? --- #26' Shinji: How can I understand... If you don't say anything? You don't say anything... You don't talk to me... How can I understand you? It's IMPOSSIBLE!! Rei: Ikari, did you try to understand? Shinji (sulking): I tried... --- and, of course #24' KAWORU: I see. Humans constantly feel pain in their hearts. Because the heart is so sensitive to pain, humans also feel that to live is to suffer. You're so delicate, like glass, especially your heart. Shinji: Me? KAWORU: Yes, you have my regard for it. Shinji: Regard? KAWORU: It means, I love you.

[]:             Quote Post by sensei_juice » Thu May 03, 2007 11:08 am Shin-seiki, I see the references, but I'm wondering if anybody had any alternate ideas as to why it makes sense that Rei and Kaworu embody these concepts.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu May 03, 2007 9:20 pm sensei_juice wrote: I prefer the second interpretation, but I recognize that views on religion are BYO here.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu May 03, 2007 11:01 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I wonder just how much they were inspired by, and in what sense they were inspired...

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu May 03, 2007 11:05 pm BrikHaus wrote: Probably along the lines of "this stuff looks and sounds cool and mysterious, so let's use it."

[]: easy             Quote Post by iloveallecchianime » Sat May 12, 2007 3:14 pm they were trying to start 3rd impact cause everyone lost hope and didnt understand each other...a condtion you must meet to love? so 3rd impact is just putting all souls into one to start over? there is a book out there explaining it all I think/
